body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
This PR removes the wind speed diagnostic from the `:long` diagnostics for the LandModel. This diagnostic is only available when running with a `PrescribedAtmosphere`, not `CoupledAtmosphere`, and including it as a default diagnostic prevents us from outputting long diagnostics when running with a `CoupledAtmosphere`. Since it's not used very often, I just removed it altogether.

Also tags v0.15.14 since we haven't made a release in a few weeks

closes #1112

### Dependency updates
```
    Updating `~/clima/ClimaLand.jl/.buildkite/Project.toml`
  [c7e460c6] + ArgParse v1.2.0
  [052768ef] ↑ CUDA v5.7.1 ⇒ v5.7.3
  [29b5916a] ↑ ClimaAnalysis v0.5.15 ⇒ v0.5.16
  [d414da3d] ↑ ClimaCore v0.14.29 ⇒ v0.14.30
  [1ecacbb8] ↑ ClimaDiagnostics v0.2.12 ⇒ v0.2.13
  [08f4d4ce] ~ ClimaLand v0.15.13 `..` ⇒ v0.15.14 `..`
  [5c42b081] ↑ ClimaParams v0.10.24 ⇒ v0.10.27
  [cd3eb016] ↑ HTTP v1.10.15 ⇒ v1.10.16
  [7181ea78] ↑ RootSolvers v0.4.3 ⇒ v0.4.4
  [0bca4576] ↑ SciMLBase v2.81.0 ⇒ v2.84.0
  [b60c26fb] ↑ Thermodynamics v0.12.10 ⇒ v0.12.11
```
title: remove LandModel wind speed diagnostic; tag v0.15.14

body: It occurred to a user wanting to make a 2 year simulation from December to December, that the last December did not have data for LAI. This was due to the latest LAI data point being in November, and not extrapolated because the next year of LAI data was not loaded. To consistently fix this issue, we add a year of data to be read to interpolate the last month with the first month of the next year. We also did this for era5 data file, where this error could also occur.
title: Grab an extra year of data for era5 and modis LAI
